title: quercus borealis f. flabellata
intro: Quercus borealis f. flabellata, also known as the paperbark white birch or paperbark birch, is a variety of the white birch tree, specifically characterized by its unique paper-like bark. The medicinal properties of Quercus borealis f. flabellata include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and even have antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus borealis f. flabellata include decoctions and infusions, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves for various health benefits.
